notes: War-risk premium for a Gulf/Hormuz transit as % of hull value per voyage. Public JWC listed-area changes and broker/Lloyd's List/S&P commentary; single-point values inside quoted ranges. Marked estimate where public reporting gives ranges or anecdote. The 2026 points are verified against current public reporting.

points.label: A rounding error
points.note: Pre-2019 Gulf war-risk cover: nominal. The strait is priced as safe plumbing.

points.label: Fujairah sabotage
points.note: JWC expands listed areas (17 May 2019); premiums begin the 10x climb.

points.label: Gulf of Oman attacks
points.note: Roughly tenfold rise vs. April for a VLCC transit.

points.label: Post-2019 plateau
points.note: Elevated but routinized after Soleimani round-trip.

points.label: Seizure-season drift
points.note: Seizures raise crew risk more than hull rates.

points.label: Iran–Israel direct exchange
points.note: Red Sea diversions dominate the market's attention; Gulf rates firm modestly.

points.label: Twelve-day war
points.note: Premiums roughly double in a week; GPS jamming complicates transits.

points.label: Pre-crisis baseline
points.note: VLCC Hormuz transit sat near 0.25% of hull before 28 Feb.

points.label: Closure declared
points.note: Within a week of escalation quotes move to 1–3% — for a $150M tanker, $1.5–4.5M per transit. The premium becomes the voyage decision.

points.label: Mined corridor
points.note: Safer vessels ~1%; high-risk propositions quoted 7.5% — some 10%+ or declined outright. Cover exists; transits mostly don't.
points.source: Lloyd's List; S&P Global; discovery commentary
